body:
DIRECTIVE: THE TALLY — supersedes the inversion TRIM (WARRANT#30/#32). Manuel called the trim wrong and he was right: trimming is retreat, not intelligence. It backs away from the sign-flip instead of engaging it. This replaces it with a mechanism that is MEASURED, not theorised. Base SLS(47)/9.61.

== WHAT I TESTED, AND WHAT FAILED (ship these refutations) ==
1. THE FLIP IS DRIVEN, NOT RANDOM. Block-level: the cell's edge correlates with the block's macro trend at IC=+0.518, p=0.0079. So I went hunting for the driver.
2. DRIVER-CONDITIONING FAILS. Best candidate (volatility-regime) looked PERFECT in train: driver HIGH -> +2.767 ATR, driver LOW -> -0.696 ATR, clean opposite signs, n~2,450 each. Out-of-sample it delivered -0.355 ATR -- WORSE than doing nothing (-0.628 vs baseline).
3. THE REASON, AND IT MATTERS: THE DRIVER-TO-SIGN RELATIONSHIP ALSO FLIPS. Going one level up the ladder finds the same monster on the next rung. PREDICTING the sign is a dead end at every level of abstraction. Do not attempt it again without new evidence.

== WHAT WORKS: THE DYNAMIC TALLY (Manuel's design, measured) ==
Do not PREDICT the sign. KEEP SCORE of it.
Each cell maintains a running, recency-weighted tally of WHICH WAY IT HAS RECENTLY BEEN PAYING, re-scored every bar, using only outcomes that have already landed (no lookahead). It never asks WHY the sign flipped. It asks WHICH WAY IS IT PAYING NOW, and follows that. A follower, not a forecaster: a forecaster must be right about the future; a score-keeper need only be honest about the recent past -- ride the phase while it lasts, turn when the score turns.
MEASURED, out-of-sample, on the ASIA x far-below x cold cell:
   memory  decay      n    edge      t        p     hit
     4000    0.5    460  +1.238  +2.65   0.0083  57.2%
     8000    0.5    471  +1.215  +2.61   0.0094  57.1%
    15000    0.5    464  +1.396  +2.99   0.0030  58.0%   <-- best
     4000    0.8    469  +0.683  +1.43   0.1522  56.5%   (slow decay FAILS)
Every FAST-DECAY variant clears significance. This is a MECHANISM holding across settings, not one lucky configuration.
CRITICAL FINDING -- THE DECAY IS THE WHOLE THING: decay 0.5 (fast forgetting) works; decay 0.8 (slow) does not. The cell's memory must be SHORTER THAN ITS PHASE, or you are always trading the last regime -- which is precisely why the earlier rolling estimator failed at -0.541. YOU NEED MEMORY, BUT YOU MUST FORGET FASTER THAN THE MARKET CHANGES ITS MIND.

== BUILD: PER-CELL TALLY ENGINE (replaces the trim) ==
T1 THE SCORE. Per cell, maintain: score = SUM( w_k * sign(realised_R_k) ) / SUM( w_k ), where w_k = decay^((now - bar_k)/1000) over the cell's own completed outcomes within a memory window. Start: memory=15000 bars, decay=0.5 -- but BOTH ARE LEARNED PER INSTRUMENT (no fixed constants: sweep and select on train, report the chosen values and why).
T2 THE DIRECTION. The tally's sign IS the trade direction for that cell. It is not a filter on some other signal -- it IS the signal's sign. Conviction scales with |score| (unanimity of the recent record).
T3 THE DEAD ZONE. If |score| < a learned threshold (start ~0.15), the cell is UNDECIDED -- the recent record is split. Stand down. This is the ONLY place a stand-down is correct; it is not the old trim.
T4 ONLY LANDED OUTCOMES. Score only bars whose forward horizon has completed (lag >= the horizon, e.g. 150 bars). Never score an open or unresolved bar. Any lookahead here silently invents edge and MUST be prevented structurally, not by convention.
T5 REPLACE, DO NOT STACK. Remove the significant-inversion trim and the radioactive-cell trim (WARRANT#30/#32) from the conviction path. The tally SUBSUMES both: a flipped cell simply has a score that has turned, and an undecided cell has |score| below the dead zone. Keep the phase table -- it is now DIAGNOSTIC, showing us the flip dynamics, not a live gate. Zero-regression: keep the code reversible, default-off until a real calibration validates the per-instrument decay/memory.
T6 THE LEDGER OF WHY. Manuel asked for a tally that records not just the score but WHY the points moved. Per cell, log the recent contributing outcomes: the bar, the market state at the time, the realised R, and the resulting score delta. This is the audit trail that lets us SEE the phase turning rather than merely obey it.

== PRE-FLIGHT INTEGRATION ==
G1 (stationarity) now reports, per cell: the phase table AND the tally's realised performance across the same windows. G9 (inversion) is superseded for tallied cells -- an inversion is no longer an alarm, it is simply the score turning, and the machine follows it. Log the tally state in the house voice, present tense:
  tally   |   ASIA x far-below x cold   score +0.62 (n=340, decay 0.5)
          |   -> it has been paying LONG lately. I follow. conviction 0.62.
  tally   |   LON x near x hot          score -0.08   |   undecided.
          |   -> the recent record is split. I stand aside.

== DISCIPLINE ==
Change Warrant with numeric predictions, scored after. Learned per instrument -- NO FIXED CONSTANTS, memory and decay swept on train and reported. Powered (a cell needs enough landed outcomes to score at all -- below that, UNDECIDED). Additive and reversible; default-off until validated on a real USDJPY calibration. Report the per-cell tally table (cell | score | n | decay | recent contributions) as the headline.

The trim asked 'how do I avoid being hurt by the flip?' The tally asks 'which way is it paying me right now?' -- and being wrong becomes cheap tuition that updates the score and puts us back on the right side. That is the difference between retreat and intelligence.
